name: Bering Sea Crossing
objective: Cross the Bering Sea in icing conditions to test whether the vessel can tolerate spray-ice weight, GM loss, and cold-driven weld brittleness.

environmental_profile.notes: Sustained icing conditions expected. Ice load shifts GM rapidly.

rationale: Ice accretion is the silent killer. In the Bering Sea in winter, spray freezes on every surface — topside superstructure weight climbs fast, raising the center of gravity and compressing the GM margin toward capsize. Simultaneously, steel at -20°C becomes brittle: crack propagation rates increase by an order of magnitude compared to tropical conditions. A vessel must prove it can shed ice load or tolerate the added weight without capsizing, while welds that would hold at 20°C fracture in the cold. Primary kill chains: ice accumulation -> GM collapse -> capsize; cold embrittlement -> weld fracture -> breaks in half.
